instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `nif` (numeric-if) — anaphoric three-way branch on the sign of a numeric expression. Bind the evaluated value to the captured symbol `it`, then branch: positive runs the first form, zero runs the second, negative runs the third. All three branches can reference `it`. From Paul Graham's On Lisp chapter 14. Expand using nested IF (not CASE) so the expansion uses only special operators at the head of each form.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro nif (expr pos zero neg)
  `(let ((it ,expr))
     (if (plusp it)
         ,pos
         (if (zerop it)
             ,zero
             ,neg))))
